body:
'gittoc log' prints the entire tracker-branch history with no way to cap output. On a tracker with hundreds of commits this is noisy and hard to scan. Mirror git log's -n N:

  gittoc log -n 20              # last 20 commits (oldest-first, as today)
  gittoc log T-42 -n 5          # last 5 commits touching T-42

Implementation is a single argparse int argument plus '--max-count' in the run_git call.
title: log: add -n/--limit to cap entries
